sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Baird Upgrades Wells Fargo (WFC)
feed.summary: Baird has upgraded Wells Fargo (WFC) from Underperform to Neutral. Analysts project an 18.57% upside for WFC, with an average one-year price target of $103.14/share. The report also details institutional ownership and fund sentiment, noting a slight decrease in the number of funds holding WFC but an increase in average portfolio weight dedicated to the stock.

feed.title: Experts say seven-year disinformation attack on Freedom Holding cost $20 million
feed.summary: Kazakhstan's media landscape has seen a seven-year-long disinformation campaign against Freedom Holding and its founder, Timur Turlov, identified by experts. This extensive negative PR effort has cost an estimated $20 million to $50 million, involving the generation of tens of thousands of negative "news" pieces, primarily on Telegram. The attack aims to damage the company's reputation and manipulate search engine results, suggesting an orchestrated effort by competitors reacting to Freedom Holding's aggressive business expansion.

feed.title: Luke Combs and Opry Entertainment Group Launch Category 10 at Universal CityWalk
feed.summary: Luke Combs and Opry Entertainment Group announced plans to open a Category 10 venue at Universal Orlando Resort's CityWalk, targeted for late 2027. This marks the third location for the brand, inspired by Combs' hit song "Hurricane," following venues in Nashville and a planned Las Vegas debut. The Orlando location will feature experiential areas influenced by Combs' music and passions, further expanding authentic country music experiences to a global audience.

feed.title: Amazon vs. Alibaba: Which E-Commerce Titan Has an Edge Right Now?
feed.summary: This article compares Amazon (AMZN) and Alibaba (BABA), the world's largest e-commerce and cloud computing companies, to determine which is a better investment. It highlights Amazon's strong momentum in retail and AWS, driven by significant investments in AI and a robust order backlog, while Alibaba faces challenges with mixed financial results, regulatory uncertainties in China, and eroding profitability despite growth in its Cloud Intelligence Group. The analysis concludes that Amazon has a clear edge due to its predictable cash flows, lower regulatory risks, and stronger financial outlook, despite Alibaba's recent stock recovery.

feed.title: Allegion (NYSE:ALLE) Stock Price Down 8.4% Following Weak Earnings
feed.summary: Allegion (NYSE:ALLE) stock fell 8.4% after reporting Q4 earnings per share of $1.94, missing the $2.01 consensus, and revenue of $1.03 billion, slightly below estimates. Despite the miss, the company increased its quarterly dividend by 8% to $0.55 and issued FY2026 EPS guidance of $8.70–$8.90, indicating confidence in future cash flow. Analysts currently maintain a "Hold" rating for the stock with an average price target of $174.25.

feed.title: How Beverly Hills advisor became Forbes’ No.1 in California
feed.summary: Lisa Detanna, Managing Director and Private Wealth Advisor at Raymond James' Global Wealth Solutions Group, has been ranked #1 on Forbes' 2026 "Top Women Wealth Advisors Best‑In‑State" list for California. This recognition highlights her exceptional leadership, client impact, and expertise in serving high-net-worth families, based on criteria like client retention, industry experience, and assets under management. Detanna, with over 30 years of experience, leads a team providing comprehensive family office support, emphasizing a deep understanding and long-term partnership with her clients.

feed.title: ServiceNow CEO IS Buying $3 Million of Stock. Executives Cancel Planned Sales.
feed.summary: ServiceNow's CEO, Bill McDermott, is purchasing $3 million worth of the company's stock, demonstrating confidence in the hard-hit software company. This move comes as other executives within the company are canceling their planned stock sales. The article highlights ServiceNow as a positive example for other software companies facing similar challenges.

feed.title: Analysts Conflicted on These Financial Names: Visa (V), American International Group (AIG) and Cullen/Frost Bankers (CFR)
feed.summary: This article summarizes analyst ratings for three financial companies: Visa (V), American International Group (AIG), and Cullen/Frost Bankers (CFR). UBS analyst Timothy Chiodo maintained a Buy rating on Visa, Mizuho Securities analyst Yaron Kinar maintained a Hold rating on AIG, and UBS analyst Zuzanna Pusz reiterated a Buy rating on Cullen/Frost Bankers. The article provides price targets, analyst success rates, and overall consensus ratings for each company.

feed.title: Parker Hannifin stock reaches all-time high at 1013.09 USD
feed.summary: Parker Hannifin's stock has hit an all-time high of $1013.09, reflecting strong market confidence in the industrial giant. The company, known for its motion and control technologies, has seen a 45.44% appreciation over the past year and recently reported Q2 2026 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations. These achievements are supported by robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and a history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: Saudi Arabia's PIF Sells Take-Two Interactive (TTWO) Stake
feed.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) has divested its entire stake in Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) as of Q4 2025, while maintaining holdings in other major tech companies. Take-Two, an interactive media powerhouse known for games like Grand Theft Auto, faces significant financial challenges including negative profitability and a distressed Altman Z-Score. The company's valuation metrics show a mixed picture, with analysts suggesting a moderate buy despite financial weaknesses and insider selling.

feed.title: Why Short Interest In The Nvidia-Heavy XLK Just Tripled While QQQ Bears Flee
feed.summary: Short interest in the State Street Technology Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLK) has nearly tripled in two months, driven by its high concentration in AI-heavy mega-cap stocks like Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. This rise suggests that institutional investors are hedging against concentration risk, rather than broadly betting against the tech sector, as short interest in the broader Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) has simultaneously fallen. The low days-to-cover ratios for XLK shorts further indicate calculated risk management rather than panic shorting.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Stock Rose 35% Last Year: Here’s Where Shares Could Go in 2026
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) stock rose 35% last year, and this article analyzes its future potential, forecasting a target price of $75 by December 2028 from its current $57, representing a 31% upside or 10% annualized return. This projection hinges on Marina Bay Sands sustaining record EBITDA and Macau reversing its 390 basis point margin compression. Key factors include CEO Patrick Dumont's leadership, Macau's market recovery, and maintaining promotional discipline.

feed.title: AI Broke the Trucks: 3 Transports to Buy After the AI Panic
feed.summary: Following an AI-induced panic in the transportation sector after a microcap company claimed its freight-optimization platform could drastically boost shipping volumes, many transport stocks fell sharply. This article highlights C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW), J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), and Union Pacific (UNP) as potentially oversold opportunities. Despite the initial market overreaction, these companies are presented as strong buys due to their entrenched market positions, operating leverage, and AI-resistant business models.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks Q2 Preview: 10th Straight Double Beat Expected — Will That Reverse Stock Slump?
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks is expected to report its 10th consecutive "double beat" for Q2 earnings, exceeding both revenue and EPS estimates, though analysts have recently lowered price targets. Investors will be watching key metrics like remaining performance obligations and annual recurring revenue, as well as updates on recent acquisitions designed to bolster its "security partner of choice" position in the AI era. This comes as the stock has slumped over the past year, making strong guidance crucial for a potential rebound.

feed.title: Citigroup Lowers Price Target for Hewlett Packard (HPE) to $26.0
feed.summary: Citigroup has maintained its "Buy" rating for Hewlett Packard (HPE) but lowered its price target from $28.00 to $26.00, a 7.14% decrease. This adjustment reflects Citigroup's updated valuation expectations for HPE amid changing market dynamics. Despite the revised price target, the "Buy" rating indicates continued confidence in HPE's future growth prospects.

feed.title: News | World's largest asset manager launches industrial REIT
feed.summary: BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager, has launched HPS Net Lease Income REIT, a private fund targeting stabilized industrial properties with long-term leases across the United States. This new REIT follows BlackRock's integration of ElmTree Funds and HPS Investment Partners last year. The fund aims to capitalize on growth opportunities in industrial net leases, driven by manufacturing reshoring, e-commerce expansion, and the need for updated warehouse infrastructure.

feed.title: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners Earnings: Nice Profitability Expansion in a Challenging Year
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) reported a 2.3% year-over-year revenue growth and a 2.7% volume growth for 2025, with an improved operating profit of 5.4% despite revenue falling below expectations. The company's full-year profitability growth exceeded forecasts due to effective pricing and efficiency. Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for CCEP to GBX 6,300 from GBX 5,400, though shares are considered about 15% overvalued.

feed.title: TD Cowen cuts IQVIA stock price target on AI concerns
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price target for IQVIA Holdings (NYSE:IQV) from $245 to $174, while maintaining a Hold rating, due to concerns over artificial intelligence's long-term impact on the contract research organization sector. This adjustment follows IQVIA's Q4 2025 results and 2026 guidance, with the new target reflecting a 13 to 14 times multiple of the firm's 2026 earnings estimate. Other analysts, including BMO Capital, Jefferies, and Evercore ISI, have also reduced their price targets, despite IQVIA reporting strong Q4 2025 financial results.

feed.title: SMCI Declines 6% in a Month: Time to Hold or Fold the Stock?
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) shares have dropped 6.4% in the last month, underperforming its industry, and now trade at a forward P/E ratio below the industry average, suggesting it might be undervalued. Despite strong AI-driven growth, SMCI faces near-term challenges including declining gross margins due to rising costs and customer concentration risks, and a surge in inventory alongside negative free cash flow. However, its high-performance servers, partnerships with NVIDIA and AMD, and expansion into Client, Edge, and Consumer AI markets provide unique advantages, leading to a "Hold" recommendation.

feed.title: Data center growth has helped PG&E cut rates 11% since 2024, CEO says
feed.summary: PG&E CEO Patti Poppe announced that accelerated large load growth, including data centers, has enabled the company to cut electric rates by 11% since 2024. However, Poppe highlighted that California's "regressive" wildfire policies are hindering affordability and credit rating improvements. She emphasized the need for legislative reform of the state's Wildfire Fund to ensure long-term infrastructure investment and lower costs for ratepayers.

feed.title: REIT Replay: Office REIT stocks plummet in recent week amid growing AI fears
feed.summary: In the past week, most US REIT indexes saw gains, with the Dow Jones Equity All REIT index rising by 4.04% and the MSCI US REIT (RMZ) index by 3.31%, outpacing the broader stock market which declined. However, office REITs bucked this trend, plummeting due to concerns that AI could reduce the need for office space. The Dow Jones US Real Estate Office index fell 6.72% to its lowest level since July 2009, with several office REIT stocks experiencing significant share price drops.

feed.title: OLB Group Stock Skyrockets On PayPal Partnership
feed.summary: OLB Group Inc (NASDAQ: OLB) shares significantly increased due to a global partner agreement with PayPal Holdings Inc (NASDAQ: PYPL). The partnership allows OLB to integrate and promote PayPal's checkout and wallet solutions, providing OLB merchants access to various PayPal services. This deal is expected to boost OLB's payment volumes and attract new merchants, with the rollout beginning in phases in Q1 2026.

feed.title: Truist Securities Lowers Price Target for Molina Healthcare (MOH)
feed.summary: Truist Securities has reiterated its "Hold" rating for Molina Healthcare (MOH) but significantly lowered its price target from $190.00 to $145.00. This adjustment follows similar price target reductions by other major financial institutions earlier in February 2026. Despite the lowered targets, the average analyst forecast still implies a 17.00% upside from the current price, while GuruFocus estimates a much larger upside based on its calculated GF Value.

feed.title: This Albemarle Analyst Turns Bullish On Improving Structural Fundamentals
feed.summary: An analyst at Bank of America Securities upgraded Albemarle's rating to Buy and raised the price target to $190, citing improving structural fundamentals. The company's strong fourth-quarter results, supported by energy storage volumes and significant cost savings, contributed to the bullish outlook. The analyst also highlighted that Albemarle's stock decline presents an attractive entry point given its execution through a downturn and positioning for recovery.

feed.title: LakePoint Sports and Coca-Cola Bottling Company United Announce Expansive Five-Year Partnership Renewal Through 2029
feed.summary: LakePoint Sports has renewed its partnership with Coca-Cola Bottling Company United for another five years, extending the collaboration through 2029. This expanded agreement will introduce new beverage equipment, self-service kiosks, and premium product offerings to enhance the guest experience at the LakePoint campus. The partnership reinforces Coca-Cola United's commitment to youth sports and community experiences, leveraging LakePoint's role as a national hub for travel and youth sports events.

feed.title: AM Best revises outlook to positive for State Street captive Federated Underwriting Company
feed.summary: AM Best has revised the outlook for Federated Underwriting Company, State Street Corporation's single-parent captive, from stable to positive, while affirming its "A-" (Excellent) Financial Strength Rating and Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating. This positive revision reflects AM Best's expectation of continued solid operating performance and close alignment with its parent company. Federated was established in 2019 to manage specific risks for State Street, benefiting from a very strong balance sheet, adequate operating performance, and appropriate enterprise risk management.

feed.title: BNY Mellon reshuffles managers on trio of municipal bond funds
feed.summary: BNY Mellon Investment Adviser, Inc. announced changes to the portfolio management teams for three of its municipal bond closed-end funds: BNY Mellon Strategic Municipal Bond Fund, Inc., BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals, Inc., and BNY Mellon Municipal Bond Infrastructure Fund, Inc. Effective February 17, 2026, Daniel Rabasco will no longer be a portfolio manager for these funds, while Jeffrey Burger will continue in his role. Thomas Casey has been added as a portfolio manager for BNY Mellon Strategic Municipal Bond Fund, Inc. and BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals, Inc., in addition to his existing role for BNY Mellon Municipal Bond Infrastructure Fund, Inc.

feed.title: Ocular claims a win with Phase 3 eye drug data even as shares sink
feed.summary: Ocular Therapeutix announced positive Phase 3 trial results for its experimental eye disease drug, Axpaxli, showing it outperformed a low dose of Eylea in treating wet age-related macular degeneration. Despite the company's claim of a successful trial, Ocular's shares dropped by 23% as analysts noted the benefits were modest and the high success rate in the Eylea control arm disappointed investors. Ocular plans to seek FDA approval, entering a competitive market currently dominated by Eylea and Vabysmo.

feed.title: Dear Oracle, we need to talk about the future of MySQL
feed.summary: A group of MySQL users and developers has proposed forming an independent foundation to guide the future of the open-source database, inviting Oracle, its owner, to participate. They express concerns about MySQL's declining popularity, lack of transparency in Oracle's development, difficulty for the community to contribute, and missing modern features. The group has given Oracle until the end of March to signal its willingness to collaborate, otherwise, they plan to proceed without the company.

feed.title: Medtronic Announces FDA approval of Infuse™ bone graft for one-and-two level Transforaminal Lumbar Interbody Fusion (TLIF) spine procedures
feed.summary: Medtronic has received FDA Premarket Approval for its INFUSE™ Bone Graft for use in one- and two-level Transforaminal Lumbar Interbody Fusion (TLIF) procedures between L2–S1. This approval makes INFUSE™ the only PMA-approved growth factor bone graft for ALIF, OLIF, and TLIF, including two-level constructs for TLIF. The decision was based on strong clinical evidence from a prospective, multicenter, randomized controlled study that showed fusion rates exceeding 90% in single-level TLIF and comparable results in two-level constructs.

feed.title: Capital One’s commercial bank zeroes in on middle market
feed.summary: Capital One's commercial bank is specifically targeting the middle market, which it views as underserved by larger banks that often shift focus to bigger corporations when lending activity increases. Neal Blinde, head of Capital One’s commercial bank, emphasized their consistent dedication to this segment, despite competition from regional banks. The recent acquisition of Brex is expected to significantly enhance Capital One's commercial payments capabilities, strengthening its offering to middle-market clients.

feed.title: Par Petroleum (PARR) Earnings Expected to Grow: What to Know Ahead of Next Week's Release
feed.summary: Par Petroleum (PARR) is expected to report a year-over-year increase in earnings and lower revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company has a Zacks Earnings ESP of +1.25% and a Zacks Rank #3, suggesting it is likely to beat the consensus EPS estimate of $1.21 per share. Investors should consider the Zacks Earnings ESP and Rank before the earnings release on February 24.

feed.title: Henry Schein (HSIC) Earnings Expected to Grow: Should You Buy?
feed.summary: Henry Schein (HSIC) is expected to report increased earnings and revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. While analysts project a 9.2% year-over-year EPS growth to $1.30 per share and revenues of $3.33 billion, the company's Zacks Earnings ESP of -0.77% combined with a Zacks Rank #3 makes it difficult to definitively predict an earnings beat. Despite a positive surprise history in past quarters, investors are advised to consider other factors beyond just earnings expectations.

feed.title: CoStar Group (CSGP) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
feed.summary: CoStar Group (CSGP) is expected to report earnings next week, with Wall Street anticipating a year-over-year increase in earnings and revenue for the quarter ended December 2025. Despite a projected earnings growth of 3.9% and revenue increase of 25.6%, CoStar's Zacks Earnings ESP is negative at -4.59% coupled with a Zacks Rank #5, making an earnings beat difficult to predict. The company has, however, consistently beaten EPS estimates in the last four quarters.

feed.title: LMT, NOC, RTX: U.S. Contractors Eye Canada’s New $6.6 Billion Defense Strategy
feed.summary: Canada has announced a new $6.6 billion defense strategy aimed at bolstering its military, supporting Canadian businesses, and creating 125,000 jobs. This initiative is drawing significant interest from major U.S. defense contractors like Lockheed Martin (LMT), Northrop Grumman (NOC), and RTX Corp. (RTX), who have historically supplied Canada with military equipment. While Canada seeks to reduce its reliance on the U.S., its small domestic defense industry likely means U.S. contractors will secure large contracts, possibly with stipulations to invest within Canada and generate local employment.

feed.title: Softbank Group dissolves share stake in Nvidia, according to SEC filing
feed.summary: Softbank Group Corp. dissolved its stake in Nvidia during the fourth quarter of 2025, according to its 13-F filing with the U.S. Securities & Exchange Commission. Following this news, Nvidia's stock price experienced a drop of up to 1.6%, trading at $179.88, which reflects the market's reaction to the disclosure of institutional investor position changes. These quarterly filings provide a snapshot of holdings at a specific date, not real-time changes.

feed.title: National Energy Services Reunited climbs more than 3% after Q4 earnings beat
feed.summary: National Energy Services Reunited (NESR) saw its stock climb over 3% following its Q4 earnings report, which surpassed analyst expectations. The company reported a 20% year-over-year increase in revenue to $283 million and an adjusted EPS of $0.18, both exceeding forecasts. NESR also highlighted robust free cash flow generation and a significant reduction in net debt, improving its leverage ratio.

feed.title: Citizens Financial To Acquire Matrix Capital Markets Group
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group Inc. announced its acquisition of Matrix Capital Markets Group Inc., a move that will strengthen Citizens' sector-focused advisory capabilities. Matrix, known for advising convenience retailers and fuel distributors, will operate as a division of Citizens JMP Securities upon closing, which is expected in the first quarter of 2026. The transaction, funded with cash, will integrate Matrix's extensive M&A transaction experience with Citizens' banking platform.

feed.title: TUI AG: Release according to Article 40, Section 1 of the WpHG [the German Securities Trading Act] with the objective of Europe-wide distribution
feed.summary: This announcement from TUI AG details a significant change in major holdings by BlackRock, Inc. According to Article 40, Section 1 of the German Securities Trading Act (WpHG), BlackRock's total voting rights in TUI AG have increased from 4.08% to 5.01%. The notification specifies the breakdown of voting rights attached to shares and through financial instruments, and provides a full chain of controlled undertakings under BlackRock, Inc.

feed.title: Scott Mezvinsky Sells 1,612 Shares of Yum! Brands (NYSE:YUM) Stock
feed.summary: Yum! Brands CEO Scott Mezvinsky sold 1,612 shares on February 13th for approximately $265,384, following a previous sale in December. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend to $0.75 per share and reported quarterly earnings, missing EPS estimates but exceeding revenue expectations. Analysts generally rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $174.00.

feed.title: Magnificent 7 stocks are down for 2 reasons in 2026. The second reason is outside their control
feed.summary: The "Magnificent 7" tech stocks (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla) are all experiencing a decline in early 2026, collectively pulling down the Nasdaq. Two primary reasons are cited, with one being within their control (capex spending on AI) and another outside their control (not yet specified in the provided text). This trend raises questions about AI spending and market risk.

feed.title: Could Your Future Tractor Run on Corn? John Deere Thinks So
feed.summary: John Deere is actively testing a 350-HP E98 ethanol tractor across the Midwest, with early trials showing promise for a cleaner, farmer-grown fuel alternative. While challenges like infrastructure and cold-weather starting exist, top executives and testing farmers believe E98 ethanol could enable a more circular production system where farmers grow their own fuel. The technology aims to eliminate the need for diesel exhaust fluid and create increased demand for corn.

feed.title: Gilead to Present New HIV Treatment and Prevention Data at CROI 2026, With a Focus on Expanding Options
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences will present new clinical and real-world data on its HIV treatment and prevention portfolio at CROI 2026. Key presentations include Phase 3 results for an investigational single-tablet combination of bictegravir and lenacapavir for HIV treatment, and further data from the PURPOSE program on twice-yearly lenacapavir for HIV prevention. The company also highlights its ongoing research into long-acting HIV therapies and efforts towards finding an HIV cure.

feed.title: What AvalonBay Communities (AVB)'s 2025 Results, Capital Returns and 2026 Guidance Update Mean For Shareholders
feed.summary: AvalonBay Communities (AVB) recently reported its Q4 and full-year 2025 results, showing increased sales and revenue but reduced net income and EPS. The company also announced new 2026 EPS guidance, completed a share repurchase, and increased its quarterly dividend. These actions emphasize AvalonBay's commitment to returning capital to shareholders, though investors will eye the 2026 guidance against ongoing regulatory and development risks in its core markets.

feed.title: Meridian Hedged Equity Fund Maintains Confidence in Vistra Corp. (VST)
feed.summary: Meridian Hedged Equity Fund, managed by ArrowMark Partners, released its Q4 2025 investor letter, noting Vistra Corp. (VST) as a major performance detractor despite the fund's continued confidence. The fund believes Vistra is well-positioned to benefit from surging electricity demand due to data centers and electrification trends, thanks to its balanced portfolio of nuclear, natural gas, and renewable assets. Vistra Corp. closed at $171.49 per share on February 13, 2026, with a market capitalization of $58.105 billion, and is held by 112 hedge funds according to Insider Monkey's database.

feed.title: Analysts Estimate EOG Resources (EOG) to Report a Decline in Earnings: What to Look Out for
feed.summary: Analysts expect EOG Resources (EOG) to report a year-over-year decline in earnings despite higher revenues for the quarter ended December 2025. The company is projected to post quarterly earnings of $2.20 per share, a 19.7% decrease, on revenues of $5.8 billion, up 3.9%. With a Zacks Rank of #4 and a negative Earnings ESP of -1.53%, the article suggests it's difficult to predict an earnings beat for EOG, while an industry peer, Diamondback Energy (FANG), shows a stronger likelihood of beating estimates.

feed.title: Novo Nordisk Expands Obesity Pipeline With New Phase 2 Trial for UBT251
feed.summary: Novo Nordisk has initiated a new Phase 2 trial for UBT251, an injectable drug aimed at treating overweight and obesity, signifying the company's commitment to expanding its obesity pipeline. This double-blinded, placebo-controlled study is designed to assess the safety, tolerability, and efficacy of UBT251. The trial is currently recruiting participants and reinforces Novo Nordisk's long-term growth strategy in the rapidly expanding obesity market, while also intensifying competition for rivals like Eli Lilly.

feed.title: Boston Scientific’s OPTION-EMEA Trial Targets One-Stop AF and Stroke Protection
feed.summary: Boston Scientific (BSX) is conducting the OPTION-EMEA clinical trial to assess the real-world outcomes of a combined procedure for patients with atrial fibrillation (AF) using their FARAPULSE PFA System and WATCHMAN FLX Pro or WATCHMAN FLX devices. This single-arm study aims to evaluate the practicality and safety of performing both AF treatment and stroke risk reduction in one visit. While the trial is still in the setup phase, successful outcomes could strengthen Boston Scientific's market position and potentially lead to increased adoption and revenue.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Rafael Carrasco Sells Shares of Waste Management I
feed.summary: Rafael Carrasco, Senior Vice President of Enterprise Strategy at Waste Management Inc (WM), sold 7,352 shares of the company on February 13, 2026, and now owns 15,539.742 shares. Over the past year, Carrasco has sold 12,965 shares and made no purchases. Waste Management Inc is currently deemed Fairly Valued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 0.98.

feed.title: Henry Schein One Integrates iTero Scanners
feed.summary: Henry Schein One has announced a direct data integration between Align Technology's iTero™ intraoral scanners and its practice management platforms: Dentrix, Dentrix Ascend, and Dentally. This collaboration aims to streamline digital scanning, diagnosis, and daily operations for dental practices globally by automatically importing iTero scans into patient records, eliminating manual steps. The integration enhances clinical confidence and operational efficiency, allowing dental teams to focus more on patient care and less on managing technology.
